channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 65 replies — 166.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-06-18 18:08 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62376829
>>62373772
Which eggregore is most predatory to us poor fags? Have our new eggregore waifu kill them

62376867
>>62376829
That's easy, it's called the grabbler.
https://youtu.be/Q4PC8Luqiws?si=wQBcUv8QOcxDJAXE

62377559
>>62376812
Drawfag days are numbered
They are gay, over-socialized and love the status quo
You would probably have better results recruiting AI "slop" artists from /g/
Despite what the sour grapes gatekeeping "artists" say, AI people put a lot of thought effort in their craft

62377563
>>62374193
BBWI

62377801
Okay guys


Here's what I have:

1. We scour the archive for leaks, gems and potential moon shots. It will be akin to searching through the dump for retro consoles. I looked through the archives and saw like 10 people invested into Palantir before it was big so I'm not too hopeful on /biz/ being the place for breadcrumbs but you never know. LINK was shilled early here.

2. We work together to look at certain patterns through events in the last 20 years. Let's not pretend reality isn't scripted. For instance! If you believed that SPACEX would rise BECAUSE Elon already had some gay scripted event with the SEC and TSLA back in '17 or '18. You would've made good money. No need for fundamentals. Just schizo and occult vibes.

3. We work on building better resumes. Lie. Lie. Lie. We find the best WFH jobs, or jobs that would NEVER hire us and "modify" our resumes to look better. Personally I hate waging and this would remind me of high school wage preparatory classes. Also this is kind of Jeety and interferes with my Weltanschauung.

4. The Drawfags idea as per
>>62373902

He will have to tell us what this Idea is and NO we are not leaving /biz/


5. The TCG pokemon way, as per >>62374140 Not a bad idea seeing as there is already a TCG general. We will have to be willing to dirty our hands with pokeniggers. If it ends poorly then maybe we can impress 18-19 art hoes with our cards.

6. We can also learn Photonics as per >>62374533 Rigorous study, I would purchase the udemy course and we all hit the books and discuss daily who has the best light tech. Probably will be boring.

Gentleman, please contribute. We need all our finest minds

GET THE FUCK OUT IF YOU HAVE MORE THAN $10,000

>>62374471
>>62374086
>>62373881

62377818
>>62377801
Also we want to pick something and stick with it. At least for 30 days to 3 months. If it isn't working then it isn't working. I don't see why ALL of us can't make it to $10k. It would also be best if we pick a route that if it doesn't work out then we at least learned something. When you invest in a shit coin and it 200x and goes to $0, what did you learn compared to someone who started an indoor shrimp farming who had a good run but had to declare bankruptcy? What REAL WORLD skills were obtained? Nothing?

62377841
>>62374042

62377846
We'll all be rich!

62377858
/biz/ is slow these days, I will check back in an hour. More suggestions would be lovely. Then we unanimously agree on something or maybe just roll for it in true 4chan fashion.

62377867
>>62377801
>He will have to tell us what this Idea is and NO we are not leaving /biz/
I am going to slowly invite drawfags from /ic/ to get at least one of them to collaborate with us and figure out how to sell art whether it be through patreon, crypto, whatever means to make a nickel. I only know of two, one of them is just beginning to draw again and doodles the cutest bears.
>>62377858
I'd like to believe a lot of us wagecuck and simply do not have the time of day but we'll make something happen.

62377880
7. We scour /smg/ as per >>62374886 and report back to this thread with the underrated, most talked about and genuinely good picks. Since there are multiple /smg/ threads this would be great.

NOTE: We should just use the current /smg/ threads and the archived ones at least within the past 1-2 years. So if you know a stock that everyone is talking about on reddit, please die. Reddit is not /smg/. Our pool that we search through will be /smg/. It would be best as it would help narrow our search and some people on /smg/ really do know their stuff. There is an individual there bragging daily about how he told everyone to buy Intel.

I am open to criticism and suggestions

62377886
>>62373752
If you actually want to get out of five figures your best bet is a six figure salary. I'm not joking, invest in yourselves.

62377896
if you have earned income and don’t have a retirement account, I would open a Roth IRA in addition to a regular taxable investment account. get in the habit of making regular contributions to both accounts. automatic deposits would be best if possible. even if you don’t make a lot, getting in the habit of investing is worthwhile. it’s best to invest in an etf like VTI or an s&p 500 etf. if there are dividends, set up automatic dividend reinvestment. (If you have a taxable account you’ll still need to pay tax on dividends, even if you reinvest them).

62378002
>>62373918
Well the /ic/ thread got deleted within minutes this is why I am cautious whenever we start to plan or congregate together its like they hate that.

62378439
>>62374533
Photonics sounds solid
I base all of the following off my schizo intuition:

1. I keep hearing about how Elon musk is a retard and SpaceX IPO is a rug pull. But you don't get that rich by being stupid.

2. I can't see X ever being profitable (though it is valuable for other reasons), and nobody gives a shit about electric cars anymore. But SpaceX... he is throwing money on that for a reason.

3. And Starlink is the world's biggest photonics deployment in space right now.
Satellite internet + photonics is going to be a huge deal
- cheap, super fast internet (terabits per second) that doesnt depend on today's bloated over-regulated infrastructure, and you can access it from anywhere
- Governments - specially EUcucks - keep making it difficult for you to use the internet and have free speech, could get BTFO the same way as the twitter trannies did when elon bought X
- Any retard shithole country with underwater drones can cut underwater internet cables, shooting down a satelite would be more difficult
- Perfect opportunity for a psyop to push people into using starlink instead of traditional intenet

If you couple that with AI companies also wanting photonics for their datacenters, you could get a lot of money this way

62378965
Brothers,
Just learn how to use the order book in polymarket, it's full of idiots that are willing to buy your bags for absurd prices. We can set our profile pictures to anime pic rel >62373866 to coordinate, target events like the current fifa event and mass shill or fud a team or group of teams so we can pump our bags
Two days ago i made 4K by just selling Belgium to win the world cup at 3x the actual price and some nigger actually bought it

62379061
>>62377886
Vague platitude

Option number 8 by >>62378965

There could be gains to be made on polymarket. Many of us are American so we'd either use kalshi or a vpn

The anon was so kind to give us an excerpt on his success and /biz/ has yet to make an impact on these markets like it did in 2021 in the BSC casino

By the end of the day, we shall have a decision set

62380691
Bump

62381251
Bump

62381343
>>62378002
/ic/ is filled with schizoids and endless seethe. And this is coming from someone who draws as a hobby.

62381797
>>62373752
>4 figure hell
unironically get a job, any job
like just work a year lol

62383107
It's settled, tune in tomorrow!

62383529
>>62373752
I am all in on SpaceX with my poorstack €6k

62383569
>>62373752
finally,a good general

62384962
Yes, leverage everything and bet it all on the moon shot. You won't regret it.

62384983
Patiently waiting for the hundred bucks of link I bought a decade ago to turn me into a millionaire.

Any day now.

62384990
>>62381797
I AM TRYING
BUT NOBODY IS HIRING

62384995
>>62378002
It's base policy to stop /ic/ from turning into a Patreon beggar spam board

62385082
>>62384990
Use your CPU to mine Nerva/XNV then. A properly decentralized crypto with no mining pools. Even a laptop works!

62385259
I bought 3 shares of spacex at 172 each should I kill myself

item.title: TUMBLING DOWN TUMBLING DOWN — 26 replies — 104.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
TUMBLING DOWN TUMBLING DOWN

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-18 18:28 UTC

OP

TUMBLING DOWN TUMBLING DOWN

Early replies (first 3)

62381997
>>62381962
Can't he just take the dividend and up it to like 20%? That should drive the price back up because people like free money right? What's stopping him?

62382004
>>62381997
not going to help if he's not going to maintain peg

62382061
To bad Saylor can't release a stable coin to pump BTC that would require DeFi lol. Tom Lee however will mention this in the next shareholder meeting.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382284
>>62382061
btc to 7k
shtlink 50.....cent - HAHAHAHA

62382319
>>62381962
the bottom is unironically $0

62382323
>>62382250
Yeah, 32 years if he sells bitcoin, but I bet that doesn't add up to the panic he will cause by a massive sell off, also liquidity, I doubt any market is ready for this retard to dump 32billion in bitcoin.

Btw, he has maybe 7-8 months of cash left to pay for dividends if he doesn't sell bitcoin or doesn't dillute his shares.

I think we can all see where this is heading to

62382325
>>62382323
54* billion

62382328
>>62382323
>7-8 months
bear market will be over by then

62382337
>>62382328
>the bear market will be over despite the biggest holder being underwater and forced to sell soon
yeah sure anon i can just FEEL the bull fomo

62382345
>>62381962
and I don't think anyone ever commented this here properly, but the impact in other protocals which relies mostly of their liquidity pool in bitcoin will be huge, imagine once people would start selling off their bitcoin or trading to any stablecoin, that will depeg as well. We had just a briefly taste of that in Luna, some other protocols that relied in Wrapped Luna in their holdings, I recall USDT, USDC depegging for a brief time and a random protocol depegging pretty bad.

I don't think people realize how in the shit this market will be if he fucks up hard.

62382352
>>62382328
I don't know, we are heading to 2 rate hikes this year and a bubble worse than .dot bursting at the same time. I don't feel comfortable at all to say that this 4 year cycle will keep untouched.

62382359
>>62382352
dotcom*

62384528
>>62382345
Usdt and usdc are backed by fixed income instruments, not saylors money magic, which like UST, is basically a derivative of Bitcoin/MSTR

62384592
>>62381997
The dividend IS almost 20% at that price lmao.

62384600
>>62381997
And pay the dividends with what money? He would have to offload a shitton of BTC. That's the crux of the issue

62384605
WERE SO BACK

62384738
>>62384600
It's called a refi, retard

62384752
>>62384600
>And pay the dividends with what money?
Saylor can go get a loan to pay for it. Ever heard of a bank?

62384761
>>62384738
>>62384752

62384809
>people bought an income product from michael saylor
DO GOYIM REALLY?

62384820
>>62381962
> Perpetual stretch
Lmao cant make this shit up

62385164
>>62381997
I believe that would just accelerate the already existing issue if the price were to continue to stay the way it is.

62385261
>>62381997
>>62385164
So let me explain what happens, once dividend raise he has more obligations to pay, that has to come from somewhere and it's from his credit. Now he has 4 means to pay his dividends from credit: 1- loans; 2 - Cash in reserve (1.1b); 4- dilluting his stocks; or 4: selling bitcoin (53B). The issue is: he has barely 9 months left to pay the CURRENT dividend yield, which I guess he accounted for something like 12% at that time. He either increses his debts (which is possible but again, refinance where?) or sell bitcoin. the problem about selling his shares of MSTR is that it both dillutes the bitcoin per share (which is good) at a cost of his shareholders, which are going to dump the price even further. The problem is that at the same time he WANTS to buy more bitcoin and it's using part of that dillution cash, so in the end mNAV (value of bitcoin per share) is not attractive to the regular investor which could simply buy Bitcoin. So basically down this path he is destroying his common stock and the premium of btc/share and exposure.

The second path which is selling bitcoin only worsens the whole ecosystem, drags price down, his reserves devalue agressively.

So that's it, both selling MSTR and BTC end up in a death spiral and both can and are happening at the same time.

62385316
>>62385261
Bitcoin is a swarm of cyber hornets.

62385351
Saylor redeemed right here and magically STRC went up.

item.title: MSTR /STRC — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
MSTR /STRC

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-18 18:18 UTC

OP

STRC is the new Terra Luna. Get ready for the rug of a lifetime.

Early replies (first 3)

62384549
>>62384536
Ok, but how will this help me buying sub1500 eth?

62384553
>>62384549
STRC collapse will force Strategy to offload BTC. Which drops the BTC price, which drops the ETH price.

Simple as

62384558
>>62384549
>sub1500 eth?

how about sub-500 eth

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62384560
>>62384549
Don't worry ethbaggie, with bitfuck dumping to sub $10k by eoy, you'll be able to increase your eth bags by buying at sub $300

62384566
>>62384553
>>62384558
>>62384560
Thats the spirit! I need that full node!

62384586
ok but what if it goes back up?

62384588
Wow I can't believe I can get an 11% APY stable coin backed by STRC this is the future of fina - AAACKK!!

62384609
Surely this 13% stablecoin is safe, 500 million dollar market cap, plus they did a livestream with Mr Saylor himself. I can't wait to retire holding my apxUSD :DDD

62384621
>>62384536
I hate this Saylornigger so much

62384628
>>62384588
>>62384609
>we refuse to learn from the luna stablecoin

62384632
>>62384588
>>62384609
keep posting kek

62384651
>>62384536
hes so fucked

62385273
>>62384536
Time to slurp this dipperino

62385298
nothing says safe fixed income product like double digit APY backed by leveraged cryptocurrency bets

62385309
>>62384560
>bitfuck
you kikes are so fucking mad lol

62385312
>>62384609
Could LINK fix the issue and get it to repeg? Looks like they just need an oracle to tell it that the price should be $1.

62385314
>>62384536
Do not worry

they have 6 million years of dividend coverage

item.content:encoded:
BING BING

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-18 18:18 UTC

OP

BING BING

Early replies (first 3)

62380382
WAHOOOOOOOOOOO

>Formerly BOOOOOOOOOOOM

62380415
>>62380373
Polkadot is /our/ crypto

62380426
Where the fuck is my charger

God speed

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62384250
>>62380382
Fuck, I would have earned so much money if I did this with HYPE instead of MSFT because "cRyPtO hAs nO YiElD". FML

62384259
>>62384250

>why yes im in my growth phase please give me an underperforming asset with worse tax liabilities

item.title: >UK less than someone in Mississippi >UK is poorer than all of 50 American states >Gets taxed 41% if you make anywhere n… — 39 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>UK less than someone in Mississippi >UK is poorer than all of 50 American states >Gets taxed 41% if you make anywhere n…

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-06-18 16:54 UTC

OP

>UK less than someone in Mississippi
>UK is poorer than all of 50 American states
>Gets taxed 41% if you make anywhere north of 40k
>Walk outside of London and it gets much worse
....

How do Europeans even live? Is all of the UK just a backwater rural wasteland outside of London?

Early replies (first 3)

62377614
>>62377608
yes lawyers make 20k in the UK lmao

62377646
>>62377608
Who cares

62377666
>>62377646
Im sure the poor sons of bitches in the UK care bro even their extremely educated make dog shit piss ass money

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62378901
>>62378898
complete bollocks. many people going to the US for work all the time and even more aspiring to.

62378927
>>62378901
Net migration is from the USA to UK dude... If you're actually successful in the UK you get a second home in some rich part of Europe like Switzerland as I mentioned, or my business partner has property in the Dolomites. America hasn't been an aspirational destination for decades, same with Australia and Canada.

62379008
I bring in £2k a month take home pay, which buys me all of nothing for a months work.
Grim and once I get my inheritance I am out of here.

62379022
>>62377608
>GDP
UK is a shithole but GDP is a retarded nonsense metric. all economists are on a one way trip to nigger hell.

62379027
>>62379022
>muh lights on choinese skycrappers and shit

62379038
>>62379027
if lights and clean streets are so cheap then why can't the so-called richest country in the world pull it off?

62379040
>>62379027
>>62379038
also the real dividing characteristic in the picture is a single high rise building vs multiple high rise buildings, a massive bridge, public transportation, etc.

62383050
>>62378898
England is abysmal, don’t be retarded. It’s only good if you are upper class, there are next to no jobs and they don’t hire white people. Everything has a retarded license attached to it, like AC, flooring, even shit like getting a drivers license costs thousands of dollars. Even public transport can take hours and is unreliable
>t. gf is British

62383053
>>62377608
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62383093
>>62377608
They're called Europoors for a reason. The sad part is they actually don't comprehend how poor they actually are. There's a bunch of post on socials of Europeans coming to America for the world cup and being blown away by everything.

62383097
>>62377608
The figure in the UK is horseshit too btw. They never include "part time" workers and often only report household figures, as though it's 1960.
Incomes in the UK are pitiful and massively uneven. If you are outside of London (and don't work on a nuclear power plant in Cumbria) then you're doing well if you make 40k. This hasn't really changed for almost two decades because our economy is so crystalline and stagnant.
Increasingly, we are turning into Russia where the capital is an economic black hole. Most of the provinces aren't simply neglected they are actively deprived because boomers erupt in rage as the prospect of distrubing their "cute" countryside retirement home. We are basically cold Florida with DC and its suburbs attached.
PPP is complete nonsense as well. Shit is expensive here it isn't like Spain or Greece where things are actually cheap. A lot of stuff is even cheaper in the US.

62383101
>>62377608
US just has more billionaires. Stop this cope. You do not deserve to feel pride in your shithole country.

62383110
>>62383101
Lmao I don't believe for one millisecond that thr average Dutch person has less wealth than the average Briton and certainly the average French person. Dutch people are more likely to own a home and housing there is about 60% more expensive. Not to mention incomes are better.
We are just very good at cherrypicking and cheating data here. It's an ordeal trying to find transparent figures for almost anything.

62383293
>>62379022

Back in 2020 in a rare chinese government report released showed that almost 1 billion chinese people earn less than $280/month, 600 million under $140/month.

62383295
>>62383293

Forgot to post pic

62383317
>>62383295
> <=0
>5,460,000 people
i wonder how many make less than 0

62383344
>>62377608
And Japan makes even less.
Yet anyone with a working brain would pick Japan over Missisipiss. Wonder why.

62383354
>>62377608
That's what happens when your empire collapses (america is next btw)

62383439
>>62383110
He is right tho don’t count the Millie’s and billies and don’t spread them out on the average, we are poor as fuck

62383476
>>62383344
in japan stuff is actually cheap tho like housing is seriously affordable whereas prices in the uk are very close to the us

62383482
Met a doctor who lived in london who literally spent half his paycheck on rent, lmao

62383533
>>62383293
and yet their home ownership rate is 95%. sounds like their purchasing power is much greater.

62383543
>>62383050
>getting a drivers license costs thousands of dollars

Lmao what? Is your gf retarded and had to take 100 driving lessons and 9 attempts at passing her practical?

62383562
>>62377608
UK is unironic hell as far as first-world countries go. No jobs, if you get a job you get paid fuck all, the education system is obtusely designed to stifle intelligent people (your path in life is determined by a decision you made at 16 which is influenced by a decision you made at 14), its tax hell, free speech doesnt exist, its turning into a surveillance state, the women are ugly and annoying, if you have kids they're gonna have miserable childhoods, its literal hell. Yookayers should leave the Yookay to literally anywhere else before Starmer shuts the borders down so nobody can leave the nightmare but everyone can come into it

62383567
>>62383543
lessons cost £35+ an hour and the average number of lessons is 45 hours (based on figures which are likely inaccurate) ontop of time spent driving with a family member etc which also costs a few hundred in insurance
it's easy to see how it would cost a few thousand

62383590
>>62383567
45 hours??? Admittedly I passed my test in 1998 but I had maybe 15-20 hours of lessons along with maybe 5 hours driving accompanied by my dad before I took my test. It was about £15/hour back then.
>>62383533
They lease everything. No one owns jack shit in China.

62383952
>>62378898
>>62383050
britain is rampant with "migrants."

62384642
>>62383590
that's the average based on the self reported number people give when they do their exam, which itself costs £63-75 plus £23 per theory which on average takes just over 2 attempts
i would sooner believe people under report this figure than over report it
>I passed my test in 1998
so as someone who drove in 1998, do you think the roads are easier or harder to drive on now?
even ignoring the changes to the test, the roads now are simply more busy and more shit than they used to be
there are paki taxis, mini roundabouts, cyclists, old people, and parked cars everywhere which there didn't used to be nearly as much
i didn't start driving until about a decade after you but even i can remember it being substantially less bad
plus they don't want people to drive so they have made it stricter which also appeals to muh safety people love so much here
if you manage it for under £2k now then you did well

62384905
>>62379022
I thought this wasn't a fair comparison, but
>Chongqing
32m ppl
>Mississippi
3m ppl

So bank overdraft fees inflate gdp by over 10x?

62384912
>>62383053
* depending on age and location

item.title: I made a stupidly autistic workbook to track and project all of my income and expenses and account balances. — 17 replies — 23.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
I made a stupidly autistic workbook to track and project all of my income and expenses and account balances.

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-18 17:48 UTC

OP

I made a stupidly autistic workbook to track and project all of my income and expenses and account balances. i have nobody else to show it to. enjoy.

the payroll calculation sheet includes the full IRS steps that my company's payroll system uses to calculate withholding and everything so i can predict nearly to the penny what my take-home pay will be given a certain number of hours worked, etc

the 401k and home equity numbers don't take growth into account, they're just starting with a recent balance and adding principal deposits only

this has had the neat effect of showing me exactly how grievous an error it is not to max out my 401k contribution up to my employer's match, because i can directly see how much net cash i'm leaving on the table (especially summed at year-end) by doing so, even if i have less liquid cash

Early replies (first 3)

62384424
So i actually like it. Shame you dont have a blank template to share with everyone to try out.

62384465
>>62384414
for predicting utility bills, i have logged all of my utility bills since we got this house in 2022 so i can predict with decent accuracy how much my energy or water bill will be for a decent month

i took all of my planned, regular transactions across all of my accounts, including all of the quarterly and yearly bills like annual insurance premiums and kids' summer camp registrations and blah blah blah and created repeating transaction series just extending outward into future dates.

so for utilities, i have planned transactions for each month that take the monthly average energy/water and then add like 30% margin and I'm always covered and usually have some gravy left over.

for day-to-day household spending, i just transfer my wife 1700 for all groceries and goods for the month (family of 5 + dog, that number is for all household goods including paper towels, detergent, baby diapers, dish soap, all that shit adds up so fast), and then put a lump sum in our joint account for 'family discretionary' expenses and gas for the cars. family discretionary is, obviously, discretionary shit like haircuts, going to the zoo, clothes for us or the kids, gifts for other people, the rare chick-fil-a trip. i don't track/project the nitty gritty there, it's just a lump allocation every month.

62384502
>>62384424
it'd take me a bit to strip my shit out, may do so if the thread is still up later. there's nothing groundbreaking here though, it's just very personalized to my favorite excel methods (tables and structured references and so forth).

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62384615
i can predict exactly how much nitpicking this screenshot will invite, but doing THIS was a colossal aid in planning, all of this shit that's non-monthly or on weird intervals and is generally hard to properly plan for. I laid it all out and then sum up the annual total, split that into a per-month set-aside (rounded up) that i auto-transfer into a separate checking account, and now this stuff never comes as a surprise.

making the transaction log was the natural consequence, where I projected these out onto future dates and plotted what the account balance will be, so i can see if i need to shore it up so it won't go negative in six months or whatever.

62384640
Thats a lot of work. Ive been able to predict my balances 4 years out to a high degree of accuracy just doing an exponential on my saving rate

62384675
>>62384414
too autistic simplify it or you'll get split focus

62384681
>>62384640
i consider myself very bad with money. and having kids has thrown a lot of disruption into the mix. i have had nearly no success with cash savings for the past few years, just spinning my wheels. as i've earned more money you can see a lot of lifestyle creep and bloat in those set-aside numbers for sure.

62384691
>>62384675
the actual interaction is, put my transactions in the list, update my predicted pay hours and then my actual payroll numbers if necessary. that's about it. this isn't for day-to-day spending, that'd be too much work to track like this.

62384712
>>62384414
I like to autistically build excel stuff too, anon kek
>picrel

62384851
>>62384712
i like it a lot. i yearn to devote some brainspace to actual work like this.

62384855
>>62384851
*actual finance work

62384955
>>62384851
Map out anything you want to build and bounce some ideas off AI and once you have your structure for whatever you're making, its off to the races with your own tweaks. I tend to just start with an idea, figure out how to build the skeleton then I start adding in different things, looking up how to add or build extras, or get it to function how I want etc, then making it as automated as possible. You clearly have skills, just a matter of figuring out what you want to build. I think I'm going to get into SQL and python next and take an excel project and turn it into a python project
>picrel
I haven't updated the stats since the beginning of the season but funnily enough the team that was ranked #1 at that point (think it was like 10-20 games in or so) ended up just winning the stanley cup

62385036
>>62384955
oh yeah execution isn't the problem, i do spacecraft shit with excel for work, what i don't know is the principles of financial/commodity analysis like your crude sheet is showing and translating that to a way to make money. i don't consider it beyond me to learn, i just haven't yet

62385071
>>62385036
>i do spacecraft shit with excel for work
that sounds pretty cool, what do you do exactly? Are you an engineer or analyst?

62385111
>>62384414
Bro just get Monarch lol

62385134
>>62385071
systems engineer, so both. On spacecraft programs I run the massive systems analysis budgets that account for the mass and power consumption of every single component on the spacecraft, and a bunch of other shit.

major use is monitoring how mass and power growth are trending as we progress through the development cycle, accounting for known-unknowns and unknown-unknowns, to make sure we don't exceed our launch vehicle capability and to make sure for the planned mission orbit the power consumption won't cause the battery to dip too low during eclipse. When the vehicle has been modeled in CAD, I pull the mass properties and predict center of mass evolution as propellant is used up and the spacecraft is in different mechanical configurations (i.e. with solar arrays and instrumentation booms deployed or stowed)

i vibe coded a cute database service with a little node.js web interface for storing the equipment data (and more importantly letting other engineers update that data with good change tracking so we can keep track of who changed what when and why), and then i have excel using power query to fetch all that data and do all the analysis. Using power query is my secret sauce and it regularly blows peoples minds when i show them this secret shit going on in the background.

62385147
>>62385111
i use You Need a Budget, I really like it, this excel stuff is primarily for forecasting and just being able to flip all my data around and look at it in different ways.

item.title: My x feed has turn into this constantly. — 19 replies — 12.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
My x feed has turn into this constantly.

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-06-18 17:01 UTC

OP

My x feed has turn into this constantly.

1) some "omfg" Claude workflow that will make you 60k/month
2) retards selling courses/coaching/mentorships in how to run a gazillion automated YT/tiktok/whatever pages with screenshots of students making money
3) various clickbait: "MrBeast shares key insights", "This guy just shared to how to make 10k a month with sleep channels", "this guy just made $2,582,285 on a memecoin"

It never ends. And now with AI the effort on having to actually write something catchy isn't there. It's just constant slop with supposed cheat codes to create slop that you can monetize.

The reality is this:
-The slop on the image makes no sense, you cannot replace a Bloomberg terminal with AI because a Bloomberg terminal gets emerging constant new sources that you don't have. Of course im not saying paying for a Bloomberg terminal is going to give you an edge and make you money. But just proving my point. Also all of these "free" workflows always have a paywall somewhere.
-Course selling scammers make money from the courses, this is not news.
-Clickbaiters make money from the traffic.

No one is making money doing something new and cool. By no one I mean, 99% of people. Everything is slop. Anyone making money is doing it from some sort of loophole. It's literally everywhere. There is always something dodgy going on.

For everything cool and genuine (like John Carmack inveting Doom in 1993 and getting rich because the game was just that good and revolutionary) 99% of people that get rich are doing it scaling slop. It will only get worse as AI gets trained on AI generated slop.

And if you try to profit from this, it's just algorithm luck to get your account ranked by the system so your posts get anywhere. Everything is literally a lottery, unless your genius is so good that it eventually thrives even if you are viewthrottled by the algorithm, but that is insanely rare.

Early replies (first 3)

62382855
>>62382838
Its always funny when people for these posts. Why the fuck would someone teach other people on ways of making easy money? Its like people selling trading bots

62382857
>>62382838
You should just stop using X. I rarely even visit this site anymore. Just start building something without all that crap bombarding your mental and you'll be better off. It's all just poison for your time.

62382861
>>62382857
There is no other place to go isn't it? I have another account that I use to find interesting tickers on semis as well as Bitcoin TA takes and basically get a news feed from the walter bloomberg guy. Everyone is on x, where the fuck do you go, facebook?
The thing is, 99% is slop, there's a few good accounts, but most is just monetized slop farms.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382867
maybe unsubscribe from slop?

62382873
>>62382838
top signal

62382878
>>62382867
you can't lol
my xitter exclusively follows gook artists because pixiv sucks but it still bombards me with what is basically ads from grifters

62382884
>>62382861
I don't go anywhere else it's all pretty much the same stuff really. I guess for "socials" I just lurk some sources I like on youtube but for the most part I'm doing my own thing and everything else is just noise. These social platforms like X are all bots and junk there isn't any real substance to any of it.

62382885
>>62382867
Im not subscribed. Even if you unfollow all these accounts, they still show up because they are accounts win tons of followers. Also there's basically no good accounts except such a few ammounts that the feed is just going to get these bigger accounts anyway. Unless you limit it all to the same few accounts you follow, but this way you close yourself to finding something new worth reading.

62382892
>>62382861
Go nowhere, put the time towards slowing down and doing nothing. Honest to god I don’t even miss YouTube anymore. My only social media is Twitch Discord and this place.

62382921
>>62382857
This. X is literally like visiting India. Here is like visiting a boomer's basement to see his millennial son.

62382933
>>62382838
Who cares.

62382980
>>62382884
So you only lurk here?
How is this place any better?
I mean sure, the fact that you cannot make money here is great, you get real people, and the bots have gone down by a lot with the new captcha it seems, but this is still a slop website

62382982
>>62382892
twitch, are we fucking serious? what do you even do there but watch livesloppers
and discord, what channels? I only chat in like 2 places

62383031
>retards selling courses/coaching/mentorships
If you are a retard and ask an LLM how to make money, it will recommend selling some type of online courses.

62383080
>>62382838
>"this guy just made $2,582,285 on a memecoin"
this one is the least believable kek
Crypto is so dead

62383308
Its like how people who buy target funds are exit liquidty for other investors and nations. The people that buy it are exit liquidity for scammers. Most scammers probably got scammed multiple times before they figured it out.

62383318
>>62382892
>Twitch
This anon pays other men to fuck his wife.

62384938
>>62382838
I-is picrel true? Is anthropic really publishing the inner workings of the financial system for free?

62384950
>>62383031
>"Claude make a course on how to make money, upload it and link it to my square account. Make it the best one"

item.title: BTC — 16 replies — 10.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-18 16:58 UTC

OP

Am I missing something here ?

Why doesn't some American millionaire just buy an entire Bitcoin and then go to Vietnam and sell it and be a literal billionaire ?

Early replies (first 3)

62383068
>>62383062
who wants to go to vietnam? it's dirty, hot, humid, and stinky

62383073
>>62383062
This is illegal. People used to do this but the goverment noticed too many people moving away and they outlawed the loophole
sorry Anon

62383077
>>62383068
>>62383073
I'm not saying they are going to live in Vietnam, but just go there and sell the coin and then fly back to the US with literal billions ?

Again, am I missing something ?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62383086
>>62383077
It's illegal I'm telling you. They do bitcoin and crypto inspections at the airports these days

62383087
>>62383077
>Again, am I missing something ?
Yes, you must declare more than 10k and the TSA will confiscate your money.

62383158
Most Vietnamese are millionaires and I am too. In fact, when i look at it closely, I was the first trillionaire

62383165
>>62383158
Then why does no one buy in the US and sell in Vietnam, 64k in the United States is not common for most to have, so selling over 1 billion in BTC in the US would be near impossible, but in Vietnam it would be really easy.

62383176
>>62383087
Why not just put the 1.6b into S&P while in Vietnam and then withdraw it when you land in the US ?

62383191
>>62383176
I'll tell you a little secret: That's how most billionaires became billionaires, it's a trick (((they))) don't want you to know about.

62383572
>>62383077
vietnam is a communist country you can't legally take money out of it

62383601
>>62383062
Don’t want to live in a third world.

62383608
The plane ride is too long to bother

62384878
>>62383062
That's literally what Arthur Hayes did back in the day, but he went to China iirc

62384921
>>62383062
that's called arbitrage, and that's something the jew SBF did to became a billionaire and fund FTX, which eventually led to his greediness alongside with his kike genes of fucking the goyim, to crash the whole market

62384925
>>62384921
become*

62384936
>>62383062

>what are conversion rates

delete this thread op this is retarded

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-18 17:52 UTC

OP

All this XRP news or banks, billionaires, movie stars using it and the price is still retarded. ITs all FAKE. XRP is supplemental liquid for banks.

Early replies (first 3)

62383846
>>62383515
Banks are using a private blockchain from ripple that doesn't need xrp banks can use their stable coin for gas instead
The only company that makes money off xrp is ripple selling it to dumb cunts that think it's needed

62384042
>>62383846
this, XRP is nothing but a funding token for Ripple who use the proceeds to establish or acquire companies/products none of which requires the use of XRP for anything, yet crippletards have not caught on after all these years still.

62385110
>>62383515
Insider here - HSBC adopting XRP, $10 incoming

62385170

item.title: >govt collects tax >its not enough >issues bonds providing interest for money >needs more money next year >tax isnt enou… — 11 replies — 5.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>govt collects tax >its not enough >issues bonds providing interest for money >needs more money next year >tax isnt enou…

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-18 16:40 UTC

OP

>govt collects tax
>its not enough
>issues bonds providing interest for money
>needs more money next year
>tax isnt enough
>issues bonds providing interest for money
>did this so many times now need debt to pay back debt

why not just NOT spend like a high end escort?

Early replies (first 3)

62379039
>>62379035
OP is a bulgarian faggot who spams this board and scams people.

62379041
>>62379039
im black and i dont spam this board

62379042
>>62379035
>why not just NOT spend like a high end escort?
the austerity necessary to balance the budget would cause an armed revolt.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62379044
>>62379035
>>62379042

62379046
>>62379035
If you are referring to the US, one of the reasons for this is there was a greater appetite for dollars outside the US than in it. However, things have changed since the dollar system was politicized.

62379058
I want the fed to raise rate to 35% and destroy the world.

thoughts?

62379071
>>62379041
worse, kys nigger

62379101
money exists to be spent. if you don't spend, there are no jobs, there is no development, there are no goods and services. the purpose of an economy is to produce things, not to hoard money. we can already see that the "free market", left to its own devices, prefers to hoard rather than spend, and uses hoarded resources to oppress and stifle growth rather than encourage it

62379304
>>62379044
Hmm those corporate taxes seem kinda low

62380467
>>62379035
Until jfk, we didn't have a deficit (he was the first one to cut taxes for economic growth). Even then, it took until Reagan for tax cuts to turn into a massive problem, with him basically halving them and double national debt in his time. Trump did the same thing (rich nigga tax cuts) and it tripled national debt, then that problem doubled under biden. The government has been collecting more of your taxes and less taxes of the people who actually have money to foot the bill. This shit is what caused the french revolution; king louis said, "we're broke dead fucking poor. Nobles, foot the bill". The Nobles said, "BITCH I'D RATHER DIE" and then they did.

We're already stripped every neccesary and not neccesary government organization and service to the bone. We can't even have free-tax filing; the problem isn't cost, it's revenue.

62384846
>>62379035
The money printer is the opiate of the state. The planet is addicted to the Rothschild smack.
